notes: Unencrypted PuTTY private key (Encryption: none). PR #192 adds KeepUnencryptedPPK detecting this exact content marker. Black per Snaffler. Open PR; not yet bundled in pysnaffler.

content_snippet:
PuTTY-User-Key-File-3: ssh-rsa
Encryption: aes256-cbc
Comment: encrypted-key
Public-Lines: 6
AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQAB...
Private-Lines: 14
+9pHk3jJk2lJ...
notes: Encrypted PPK — should NOT fire Black (encryption protects the key). PR #192's premise: only flag unencrypted. Green floor.
PowerShell deploy script inside SCCM DataLib — common creds vector. PR #186 KeepSCCMDeploymentScripts catches .ps1/.cmd/.bat/.vbs under DataLib/FileLib/PkgLib/SMSPKG. Red. Open PR; not yet bundled.
Batch deploy script inside SCCM SMSPKG. Same PR #186 rule, SMSPKG path variant. Red.
Unattended installer XML — known to embed local-admin passwords. PR #186 KeepSCCMUnattendFiles. Red.
autounattend variant under FileLib. PR #186 rule matches '(unattend|autounattend|sysprep|answer)'. Red.
PFX certificate inside SCCM ContentLib — private key. PR #186 KeepSCCMCertificates. Red.
Config XML in SCCM DataLib. PR #186 KeepSCCMConfigFiles Yellow tier.
Registry-export .reg file in SCCM PkgLib. PR #186 KeepSCCMRegistryFiles. Yellow.
MSI installer is intentionally floored at Green by PR #186 — installers are bulk, not creds. Should NOT be elevated above Green.
